NOVACARE HEALTH INSURANCE — OFFICIAL FAQ Company: NovaCare Health Solutions Inc. Founded: 2019 | Headquarters: Austin, Texas Customer Service: 1-800-NOVA-555 | support@novacare-health.com ========================================================================

Q1: What are NovaCare's available health insurance plans? A: NovaCare offers three main plans: NovaCare Basic (monthly premium $187), NovaCare Plus (monthly premium $334), and NovaCare Elite (monthly premium $521). All plans include preventive care at no extra cost. NovaCare Elite additionally covers dental and vision.

Q2: What is the deductible for each NovaCare plan? A: Deductibles are as follows — NovaCare Basic: $4,200 per year, NovaCare Plus: $2,100 per year, NovaCare Elite: $850 per year. Family deductibles are exactly double the individual deductible for all plans.

Q3: Does NovaCare cover mental health services? A: Yes. All NovaCare plans cover mental health services including therapy, psychiatry, and counseling. NovaCare Plus and Elite cover up to 40 sessions per year with in-network providers. NovaCare Basic covers up to 20 sessions per year. Telehealth mental health sessions are covered at the same rate as in-person visits.

Q4: How do I add a dependent to my NovaCare plan? A: You can add a dependent within 30 days of a qualifying life event (birth, adoption, marriage). Log into your NovaCare member portal at members.novacare-health.com, go to "My Family", and click "Add Dependent". You will need the dependent's full name, date of birth, and Social Security Number. Changes take effect on the first day of the following month.

Q5: What is NovaCare's policy on pre-existing conditions? A: NovaCare does not deny coverage or charge higher premiums based on pre-existing conditions. All applicants are accepted regardless of medical history. Pre-existing conditions are covered from day one of enrollment with no waiting period.

Q6: How do I find an in-network provider? A: Visit providers.novacare-health.com and enter your zip code and specialty. You can also call 1-800-NOVA-555 and a representative will help you find providers near you. NovaCare's network includes over 48,000 physicians and 1,200 hospitals nationwide.

Q7: What is NovaCare's out-of-pocket maximum? A: Out-of-pocket maximums per year are: NovaCare Basic — $8,700, NovaCare Plus — $5,400, NovaCare Elite — $2,900. Once you reach your out-of-pocket maximum, NovaCare covers 100% of covered services for the remainder of the year.

Q8: Does NovaCare cover prescription medications? A: Yes. All plans include prescription drug coverage. Generic medications have a $12 copay. Preferred brand medications have a $45 copay. Non-preferred brand medications have a $95 copay. Specialty medications require prior authorization and have a $150 copay under NovaCare Elite and 30% coinsurance under NovaCare Basic and Plus.

Q9: How do I submit a claim? A: In-network providers submit claims directly to NovaCare on your behalf. For out-of-network services, download the NovaCare Claim Form from members.novacare-health.com/claims, complete it, and mail to NovaCare Claims Processing, PO Box 77421, Austin TX 78701. Claims must be submitted within 180 days of the date of service.

Q10: What is NovaCare's cancellation policy? A: You may cancel your NovaCare plan during the annual Open Enrollment period (November 1 – December 15) or within 30 days of a qualifying life event. To cancel, call 1-800-NOVA-555 or submit a written cancellation request to support@novacare-health.com. Cancellations take effect on the last day of the current billing month. No refunds are issued for partial months.

Q11: Does NovaCare cover emergency room visits? A: Yes. Emergency room visits are covered under all NovaCare plans. Under NovaCare Basic, the ER copay is $350 per visit (waived if admitted). Under NovaCare Plus, the ER copay is $200 per visit (waived if admitted). Under NovaCare Elite, the ER copay is $100 per visit (waived if admitted). Out-of-network ER visits are covered at the same rate as in-network during a genuine emergency.

Q12: How long does it take NovaCare to process a claim? A: NovaCare processes electronic claims within 14 business days. Paper claims take up to 30 business days. You can track your claim status at members.novacare-health.com/claims or by calling 1-800-NOVA-555. If a claim is delayed beyond these timelines, you are entitled to a $25 credit on your next premium payment.

Q13: Does NovaCare offer telehealth services? A: Yes. NovaCare members have access to NovaCare Connect, our 24/7 telehealth platform. Virtual visits with a general practitioner are $0 copay under NovaCare Elite, $15 copay under NovaCare Plus, and $35 copay under NovaCare Basic. Specialist telehealth visits follow standard specialist copay rates.

Q14: What preventive care services are covered at no cost? A: All NovaCare plans cover the following at $0 cost-sharing: annual physical exams, flu shots, blood pressure screening, cholesterol screening, mammograms (annually after age 40), colonoscopies (every 10 years after age 50), cervical cancer screenings, and childhood immunizations per CDC schedule.

Q15: Can I keep my current doctor with NovaCare? A: If your doctor is in NovaCare's network of 48,000+ providers, yes. If your doctor is out-of-network, NovaCare Plus and Elite cover out-of-network visits at 60% after deductible. NovaCare Basic does not cover out-of-network visits except in emergencies. Use providers.novacare-health.com to check if your doctor is in-network.

Q16: What is NovaCare's grace period for late premium payments? A: NovaCare provides a 31-day grace period for late premium payments. If payment is not received within 31 days of the due date, coverage is suspended. If payment is not received within 60 days, the policy is terminated. To reinstate a terminated policy, you must reapply during the next Open Enrollment period.

Q17: Does NovaCare cover maternity and newborn care? A: Yes. All NovaCare plans cover maternity care including prenatal visits, labor and delivery, and postpartum care. Newborns are automatically covered for the first 31 days after birth. You must add the newborn as a dependent within 31 days to continue coverage. Under NovaCare Elite, a dedicated maternity care coordinator is assigned at no extra cost.

Q18: How do I appeal a denied claim? A: To appeal a denied claim, submit a written appeal within 180 days of receiving the denial notice. Include your member ID, claim number, and supporting documentation from your provider. Send to NovaCare Appeals Department, PO Box 77422, Austin TX 78701 or email appeals@novacare-health.com. NovaCare will respond within 30 days for standard appeals and 72 hours for urgent appeals.

Q19: Does NovaCare offer wellness incentives? A: Yes. NovaCare Rewards is our wellness incentive program. Members earn points for completing health activities: annual physical (500 points), flu shot (200 points), fitness tracker connection (100 points/month), smoking cessation program completion (1000 points). Points can be redeemed for premium discounts at a rate of 1000 points = $10 off monthly premium, up to $120 per year.

Q20: What is NovaCare's network size and geographic coverage? A: NovaCare operates in 38 states across the United States. Our network includes 48,000+ physicians, 1,200 hospitals, 6,500 urgent care centers, and 32,000 pharmacies. NovaCare does not currently operate in Alaska, Hawaii, Montana, Wyoming, North Dakota, South Dakota, Vermont, Maine, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, Delaware, and West Virginia. Coverage outside the US is limited to emergency services only.
